title: alnus rubra
intro: Alnus rubra, also known as the Red Alder, is a deciduous tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The Red Alder has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Red Alder include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Red Alder are typically made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, colds, and flu.
